actionUnits.condition: MonoBehaviour_0 (m_Script guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) is the Fire script attached to this object. Scene config matches script fields: debug=0, isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, maxHitInterval=2, totalHitTime=5, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, downMultiplier=2, mainEffectGo=fileID 1849464941111748095. Object has BoxCollider (IsTrigger=0) and no Rigidbody. There are no UnityEvent m_Calls configured. This Trigger represents a single water hit invoking IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(normal) once via the water system colliding with this collider, setting isTurningOff=true and currentHitInterval reset.

Sustain repeated water hits (call WaterHit(normal) multiple times with intervals < maxHitInterval=2s) so that accumulated currentHitTime >= totalHitTime=5s, causing the fire to turn off (isOn=false) and UpdateFireState(true) to notify neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1).

Ensure at least onNeighborsThreshold=2 neighbor Fire instances within maxNeighborDistance=4 are on, and at least one is lower than this fire by > minNeighborDownDistance=3 to exercise downMultiplier=2. This allows shouldTurnOn to become true and, after timeToOn randomly between 40–60s (set in Awake), fire auto-ignites (isOn=true) and UpdateFireState(true) notifies neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1).
